text: : مَنْ قَرَأَ سُورَهَ الْحَجِّ فِی کُلِّ ثَلَاثَهِ أَیَّامٍ لَمْ تَخْرُجْ سَنَتُهُ حَتَّی یَخْرُجَ إِلَی بَیْتِ اللَّهِ الْحَرَامِ وَ إِنْ مَاتَ فِی سَفَرِهِ دَخَلَ الْجَنَّهَ قُلْتُ فَإِنْ کَانَ مُخَالِفاً قَالَ یُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُ بَعْضُ مَا هُوَ فِیهِ.

en.sayed-athar-husain-rizvi-&-sayed-maqs: 1 - In this chain of narration, from Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Suraqa, from his father, from Abu Abdullah (Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (as)), he said: "Whoever recites Surah Al-Hajj every three days, his age will not end until he goes to the Sacred House of Allah (Kaaba), and if he dies during his journey, he will enter paradise." I asked, "What if he is unable to do so?" He replied, "Some of the difficulty will be alleviated for him."
